title: rubus subalpestris
intro: Rubus subalpestris, also known as alpine bramble or subalpine raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that grows in high-altitude mountainous regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus subalpestris include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Rubus subalpestris is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and may also be used in salves and creams for topical application.
